### Nanoscale

## Preparation of functional magnetic nanocomposites and hybrid materials: recent progress and future directions

\* 

Corresponding authors

a

Karlsruhe Institute of Technology, Institute for Technical Chemistry, Karlsruhe, Germany
  
**E-mail:**
[silke.behrens@kit.edu](mailto:silke.behrens@kit.edu "silke.behrens@kit.edu")   
**Fax:** +49 7247 822244

### Abstract

The aim of this article is to provide an overview of current research activities on functional, magnetic nanocomposite materials. After a brief introduction to general strategies for the synthesis of superparamagnetic nanoparticles (NPs), different concepts and state-of-the-art solution chemical methods for their integration into various types of functional, magnetic nanocomposite materials will be reviewed. The focus is on functional materials which are based on discrete magnetic NPs, including multicomponent nanostructures, colloidal nanocrystals, matrix-dispersed composite materials and mesoscaled particles. The review further outlines the magnetic, structural, and surface properties of the materials with regard to application.

![Graphical abstract: Preparation of functional magnetic nanocomposites and hybrid materials: recent progress and future directions](/en/Image/Get?imageInfo.ImageType=GA&imageInfo.ImageIdentifier.ManuscriptID=C0NR00634C&imageInfo.ImageIdentifier.Year=2011)![](https://www.rsc-cdn.org/pubs-core/2022.0.188/content/NewImages/Ajax-GA-Loader.gif)

enriched_points.expanded_content: Research should focus on synthesizing innovative nanomaterials, such as hybrid organic-inorganic composites, to enhance functionality and application versatility in various fields.
Efforts must be made to improve the performance traits of current composites, balancing functionality and stretchability, which can lead to breakthroughs in practical applications.
Integrating knowledge from chemistry, physics, and engineering can foster novel designs and applications for nanocomposites, enhancing their effectiveness across multiple domains.
Partnerships with industry stakeholders can accelerate the translation of research findings into commercial products, ensuring that functional nanocomposites meet real-world needs.
